test_cases.input: Our Q1 revenue was $50 million, up 15% year-over-year. We launched the new AI platform last month. Customer retention improved to 92%. We expect continued growth in Q2 with projected revenue of $55 million.

The company maintains a 99% confidence level that the maximum loss will not exceed $5 million in the next trading day, with a robust tier one capital ratio of 12.5%. Forecasts for the next quarter predict revenue of $135 million, a 8% quarter-over-quarter growth, driven by blockchain solutions and AI analytics, and an anticipated $200 million IPO from PayPlus subsidiary, promising enhanced liquidity and growth opportunities.

We completed the migration to Kubernetes last Friday. The new system handles 10,000 requests per second with 99.99% uptime. Latency dropped from 200ms to 45ms. The team plans to add auto-scaling by end of month.
The meeting was rescheduled to next Tuesday.

Welcome everyone to today's quarterly review. I'll be covering our financial performance, operational highlights, and strategic outlook. Let's start with the numbers. Revenue came in at $42 million for the quarter, which represents a 12% increase from last quarter. Our operating margin improved to 18%, up from 15% last quarter. The main driver was our enterprise segment, which grew 25% to $28 million. On the cost side, we kept operating expenses flat at $34 million despite hiring 15 new engineers. Looking ahead, we expect Q2 revenue between $45-48 million. We're also on track for the PayPlus IPO in Q3, which we expect to raise approximately $200 million. Any questions?
